# Stone.js

> The Continuum framework. Write your domain once; it lives in superposition across every execution context (server, edge, browser, agents) and collapses into one at runtime. Stone.js is the reference implementation of the Continuum Architecture: an application is an act, Domain times Context resolves into a Resolution. You write the domain; adapters supply the context at runtime, so the same code runs on Node, AWS Lambda, Cloudflare, Deno, Bun, the browser, and AI agents.

Two paradigms are supported at strict parity: declarative (TC39 stage-3 decorators with Symbol.metadata) and imperative (define* meta-modules). Three forms everywhere: class, factory, function (the function form never receives the container; providers forbid it).

## Start here
- [Why Stone.js](/docs): an application is an act, not an object; the thesis in three minutes.
- [Install and create](/docs/start/install): scaffold with npm create @stone-js, then run locally.
- [Your first domain](/docs/start/first-domain): write the what (a service and a handler) with no reference to where it runs.
- [Collapse it](/docs/start/collapse): choose the runtime by adding an adapter to the manifest, not by editing the domain.
- [Project anatomy](/docs/start/anatomy): what the scaffold creates and why.

## Foundations (the architecture; principle then In Stone.js)
- [The Continuum](/docs/foundations/continuum): the app as an act; the four dimensions (Setup, Integration, Initialization, Functional).
- [Domain times Context resolves into Resolution](/docs/foundations/domain-context): keep the domain pure, let the context arrive.
- [The uncertainty principle](/docs/foundations/uncertainty): defer the where, own the what.
- [Superposition and collapse](/docs/foundations/superposition): stack adapters; the collapse happens at runtime.
- [The ephemeral context](/docs/foundations/ephemeral-context): a fresh container per event; only the adapter is long-lived.
- [Blueprint: config as a manifest](/docs/foundations/blueprint): one immutable manifest, addressed by dotted stone.* keys.
- [Blueprint as a pipeline](/docs/foundations/build-phase): the manifest is composed by a pipeline of steps.
- [Lifecycle and the kernel](/docs/foundations/lifecycle): the kernel applies the domain per event; @Hook lifecycle points.
- [Middleware and the pipeline](/docs/foundations/middleware): the chain of responsibility, applied to events.
- [Service container and DI](/docs/foundations/container): resolve and inject services by alias.
- [Service providers](/docs/foundations/providers): register then boot; the seam every module plugs into.
- [Adapters](/docs/foundations/adapters): the integration dimension; capture, normalise, emit.
- [The two paradigms](/docs/foundations/paradigms): decorators and define* at parity, one Blueprint.
- [The three forms](/docs/foundations/forms): class, factory, function.

## Contexts (the dimensions; the same domain, collapsed differently)
- [Backend](/docs/contexts/backend): Node HTTP and CLI adapters on one domain.
- [Frontend](/docs/contexts/frontend): React pages and layouts; CSR, SSR, SSG.
- [Edge and Serverless](/docs/contexts/edge): one Web-standard adapter for Cloudflare, Deno, Bun, Vercel, Netlify; plus AWS Lambda.
- [Agents](/docs/contexts/agents): expose the domain as MCP tools.
- [Mobile](/docs/contexts/mobile): a forthcoming context (React Native / Expo) as one more adapter.

## Essentials (the runtime building blocks, in detail)
- [Application and bootstrap](/docs/essentials/application): the manifest, adapters, app options.
- [Event handlers](/docs/essentials/event-handlers): the handler contract; the three forms.
- [Incoming event](/docs/essentials/incoming-event): reading input via event.get; HTTP getters; files.
- [Outgoing response](/docs/essentials/outgoing-response): return data or build a response; helpers.
- [Cookies](/docs/essentials/cookies): read and set cookies with secure options.
- [Configuration](/docs/essentials/configuration): set and read stone.* keys on the Blueprint.
- [Environment](/docs/essentials/environment): typed, validated env getters.
- [Events and listeners](/docs/essentials/events): emit domain events; listeners and subscribers.
- [Error handling](/docs/essentials/errors): throw meaningful errors; map them to responses.
- [Logging](/docs/essentials/logging): the logger, levels, custom backend.
- [Hooks and lifecycle](/docs/essentials/hooks): run code at startup, shutdown and around each event.

## Dependency injection
- [The container](/docs/di): bindings and resolution; singleton/instance/alias/resolve/make/factory.
- [Services](/docs/di/services): @Service / defineService; aliases; singletons; injection by alias.
- [Service providers](/docs/di/providers): register then boot; mustSkip; how modules attach.

## Blueprint & build
- [The Blueprint](/docs/blueprint): the keyed, immutable manifest; get/set/has/add.
- [Blueprint middleware](/docs/blueprint/middleware): the build pipeline; insert steps to refine the manifest.
- [Meta-modules and define*](/docs/blueprint/meta-modules): the imperative model; the define* family.
- [The build and targets](/docs/blueprint/build): module discovery, .stone codegen, targets, stone.config.

## Middleware
- [Overview](/docs/middleware): the pipe model (in via next, out on the response).
- [Defining middleware](/docs/middleware/defining): class/factory/function; configurable middleware.
- [Registering middleware](/docs/middleware/registering): global/group/route; priority, alias, excludeMiddleware.
- [Terminating middleware](/docs/middleware/terminating): shape the response and run cleanup on the way out.

## Routing (dedicated section)
- [Overview](/docs/routing): the universal router, the mental model, section map.
- [Route definitions](/docs/routing/definitions): verb decorators, controllers, defineRoutes, options.
- [Parameters and constraints](/docs/routing/parameters): path params, optional, catch-all, rules.
- [Query and body](/docs/routing/query-body): reading input via event.get, one accessor.
- [Model binding](/docs/routing/binding): resolve a param into a model at the boundary.
- [Groups and prefixes](/docs/routing/groups): shared base path and middleware via controllers/children.
- [Named routes and URL generation](/docs/routing/names): name routes, router.generate.
- [Route middleware](/docs/routing/middleware): attach middleware per route/group, order, excludeMiddleware.
- [Redirects and responses](/docs/routing/redirects): returning data, status, redirects.
- [Matching and precedence](/docs/routing/matching): how a route is chosen; domain/protocol/strict.
- [Misc](/docs/routing/misc): fallback, current route, useRouter, routing in the browser.

## Build (recipes)
- [Validation](/docs/build/validation): one schema, enforced on the API and the form.
- [Auth and authorization](/docs/build/auth): stateless JWT/OAuth; isomorphic RBAC and ABAC.
- [Resources and OpenAPI](/docs/build/resources): shape the output; derive the public contract.
- [Testing](/docs/build/testing): boot the real app in memory; dispatch real events.

## Frontier (advanced)
- [Write your own adapter](/docs/frontier/adapter): open a new context in three verbs.
- [Universal apps](/docs/frontier/universal): SSR as a superposition of browser and server.
- [Agent-native patterns](/docs/frontier/agent-native): design a domain machines can use well.

## Ecosystem
- [Marketplace](/docs/ecosystem): the full catalog, grouped by role in the architecture.

## Reference
- [API](/docs/reference/api): primary entry points per package, and the generated TypeDoc reference at /api.
- [CLI](/docs/reference/cli): the stone command line.
- [Configuration](/docs/reference/config): the stone.* keys.
- [Glossary](/docs/reference/glossary): the Continuum vocabulary.

## Optional
- [The Continuum manifesto](https://evens-stone.github.io/continuum-manifesto/manifesto): the philosophy behind the framework.
